---
title: "Section 14: `lubridate`"
author: "Matt Higham"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```

Class Exercises

Class Exercise 1.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
ds_df <- read_csv(here("data/ds_google.csv"))
ds_df

ds_df <- ds_df |> mutate(search_date = ym(Month))

ggplot(data = ds_df, aes(x = search_date, y = Data_Science)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_smooth()
```

Class Exerciswe 3.

```{r}
library(tidyquant)
stocks_long <- tq_get(c("AAPL", "CMG", "NTDOY", "SPY", "GME"), get = "stock.prices")
stocks_gme <- stocks_long |> filter(symbol == "GME")
ggplot(data = stocks_gme, aes(x = date, y = close)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_minimal()

```

Class Exercise 4.

```{r}
tibble(date = c("2024-01-01", "2024-01-02", "2024-01-03"),
       price = c(31, 40, 20)) |>
  mutate(lag_price = lag(price))

## hints:
## use lag()
## for parts 2 and 3, use lubridate functions
```

```{r}
stocks_spy <- stocks_long |> filter(symbol == "SPY")
stocks_spy |> mutate(lag_close = lag(close)) |>
  relocate(close, lag_close) |>
  mutate(is_higher = if_else(close > lag_close, true = 1, false = 0)) |>
  relocate(is_higher) |>
  summarise(prop_higher = mean(is_higher, na.rm = TRUE))
## during this span, the S and P was higher the day after about 55% of the time.

## week to week
stocks_spy |> mutate(weekday = wday(date, label = TRUE)) |>
  filter(weekday == "Wed") |>
  mutate(lag_close = lag(close)) |>
  relocate(close, lag_close) |>
  mutate(is_higher = if_else(close > lag_close, true = 1, false = 0)) |>
  relocate(is_higher) |>
  summarise(prop_higher = mean(is_higher, na.rm = TRUE))
## 0.629

## month to month
stocks_spy |> mutate(day_of_month = mday(date)) |>
  filter(day_of_month == 15) |>
  mutate(lag_close = lag(close)) |>
  relocate(close, lag_close) |>
  mutate(is_higher = if_else(close > lag_close, true = 1, false = 0)) |>
  relocate(is_higher) |>
  summarise(prop_higher = mean(is_higher, na.rm = TRUE))
## this is flawed if the 15th falls on a weekend, your lag will be too long

stocks_spy |> mutate(day_of_month = mday(date),
                     year = year(date),
                     month = month(date)) |>
  group_by(month, year) |>
  slice(1) |>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(date) |>
  mutate(lag_close = lag(close)) |>
  relocate(close, lag_close) |>
  mutate(is_higher = if_else(close > lag_close, true = 1, false = 0)) |>
  relocate(is_higher) |>
  summarise(prop_higher = mean(is_higher, na.rm = TRUE))
```
